children.heading: Account
children.label: 1 Account
children.text: The term “account” means a deposit account at a bank.

children.label: A
children.text: any depository institution (as defined in );section 461(b)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: any Federal reserve bank;

children.label: C
children.text: any Federal home loan bank; or

children.label: i
children.text: the Treasury of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: the United States Postal Service;

children.label: iii
children.text: a State government; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a unit of general local government (as defined in ).section 4001(24) of this title

children.heading: Collecting bank
children.label: A Collecting bank
children.text: The term “collecting bank” means any bank handling a check for collection except the paying bank.

children.label: i
children.text: the first bank to which a check is transferred, even if such bank is also the paying bank or the payee; or

children.label: ii
children.text: a bank to which a check is transferred for deposit in an account at such bank, even if the check is physically received and indorsed first by another bank.

children.label: i
children.text: the bank by which a check is payable, unless the check is payable at or through another bank and is sent to the other bank for payment or collection; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the bank at or through which a check is payable and to which the check is sent for payment or collection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “returning bank” means a bank (other than the paying or depositary bank) handling a returned check or notice in lieu of return.

children.heading: Treatment as collecting bank
children.label: ii Treatment as collecting bank
children.text: No provision of this chapter shall be construed as affecting the treatment of a returning bank as a collecting bank for purposes of section 4–202(b) of the Uniform Commercial Code.

children.heading: Board
children.label: 4 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.

children.heading: Business day
children.label: 5 Business day
children.text: The term “business day” has the same meaning as in .section 4001(3) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: means a draft, payable on demand and drawn on or payable through or at an office of a bank, whether or not negotiable, that is handled for forward collection or return, including a substitute check and a travelers check; and

children.label: B
children.text: does not include a noncash item or an item payable in a medium other than United States dollars.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to a check handled for forward collection, draws the check on a consumer account; or

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to a check handled for return, deposits the check into, or cashes the check against, a consumer account.

children.heading: Consumer account
children.label: 8 Consumer account
children.text: The term “consumer account” has the same meaning as in .section 4001(10) of this title

children.heading: Customer
children.label: 9 Customer
children.text: The term “customer” means a person having an account with a bank.

children.heading: Forward collection
children.label: 10 Forward collection
children.text: The term “forward collection” means the transfer by a bank of a check to a collecting bank for settlement or the paying bank for payment.

children.heading: Indemnifying bank
children.label: 11 Indemnifying bank
children.text: The term “indemnifying bank” means a bank that is providing an indemnity under with respect to a substitute check.section 5005 of this title

children.heading: MICR line
children.label: 12 MICR line
children.text: The terms “MICR line” and “magnetic ink character recognition line” mean the numbers, which may include the bank routing number, account number, check number, check amount, and other information, that are printed near the bottom of a check in magnetic ink in accordance with generally applicable industry standards.

children.heading: Noncash item
children.label: 13 Noncash item
children.text: The term “noncash item” has the same meaning as in .section 4001(14) of this title

children.heading: Person
children.label: 14 Person
children.text: The term “person” means a natural person, corporation, unincorporated company, partnership, government unit or instrumentality, trust, or any other entity or organization.

children.label: A
children.text: the bank that creates a substitute check; or

children.label: B
children.text: if a substitute check is created by a person other than a bank, the first bank that transfers or presents such substitute check.

children.label: A
children.text: contains an image of the front and back of the original check;

children.label: B
children.text: bears a MICR line containing all the information appearing on the MICR line of the original check, except as provided under generally applicable industry standards for substitute checks to facilitate the processing of substitute checks;

children.label: C
children.text: conforms, in paper stock, dimension, and otherwise, with generally applicable industry standards for substitute checks; and

children.label: D
children.text: is suitable for automated processing in the same manner as the original check.

children.heading: State
children.label: 17 State
children.text: The term “State” has the same meaning as in .section 1813(a) of this title

children.heading: Truncate
children.label: 18 Truncate
children.text: The term “truncate” means to remove an original paper check from the check collection or return process and send to a recipient, in lieu of such original paper check, a substitute check or, by agreement, information relating to the original check (including data taken from the MICR line of the original check or an electronic image of the original check), whether with or without subsequent delivery of the original paper check.

children.heading: Uniform Commercial Code
children.label: 19 Uniform Commercial Code
children.text: The term “Uniform Commercial Code” means the Uniform Commercial Code in effect in a State.

children.heading: Other terms
children.label: 20 Other terms
children.text: Unless the context requires otherwise, the terms not defined in this section shall have the same meanings as in the Uniform Commercial Code.
